zh.lifeSummary: Lord Peter Fitz Henry是Henry FitzAilwin Mayor of London和Margaret Fitz-Ailwin的兒子，於1128年出生在英國米德萊可倫敦。他於1178年與Isabel De Chesney在英國素瑞阿丁頓,第一代西德默斯子爵結婚。他們至少是4個女兒的父母。他去世於1207年10月30日在英國素瑞阿丁頓,第一代西德默斯子爵享年79歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lord Peter Fitz Henry是Henry FitzAilwin Mayor of London和Margaret Fitz-Ailwin的儿子，于1128年出生在英国米德萊可倫敦。他于1178年与Isabel De Chesney在英国素瑞阿丁頓,第一代西德默斯子爵结婚。他们至少是4个女儿的父母。他去世于1207年10月30日在英国素瑞阿丁頓,第一代西德默斯子爵享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, Walloon, and West Indian (mainly Jamaica and Haiti): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements haim, heim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’, introduced to England by the Normans in the form Henri. During the Middle Ages this name became enormously popular in England and was borne by eight kings. Continental forms of the personal name were equally popular throughout Europe. In the period in which the majority of English surnames were formed, a common English vernacular form of the name was Harry, hence the surnames Harris (southern) and Harrison (northern). Official documents of the period normally used the Latinized form Henricus. In medieval times, English Henry absorbed an originally distinct Old English personal name that had hagan ‘hawthorn’ (compare Hain 2) as its first element, and there has also been confusion with Amery. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. German Heinrich , and also their derivatives, e.g. Swedish Henriksson (see Henrikson ). Compare Henri .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hInnéirghe ‘descendant of Innéirghe’, a byname based on éirghe ‘arising’.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Éinrí or Mac Einri, patronymics from the personal names Éinrí, Einri, Irish forms of Henry. It is also found as a variant of McEnery .

variants.name: Guccio
meanings: Italian: from the late medieval personal name Guccio, a shortened pet form of any of various names ending in -go, for example Arrigo (see Henry ) or Ugo (see Hugh ).

variants.name: Arrigo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Pasquale, Salvatore, Sal, Santo, Vincenzo, Antonino, Franco, Mario, Natale, Vito.
Italian: from the medieval personal name Arrigo, a variant of Enrico (see Henry ).

variants.name: Arch
meanings: English: topographic name for someone living by a bridge, from Middle English, Old French arche ‘arch’ (i.e. of a bridge).
English (of Norman origin): shortened form of Arches, a habitational name from any of various places in France called Arques (Eure, Pas-de-Calais, Seine-Maritime).
Germanized form of Slovenian Arh: from a shortened form of the personal name Areh, an old vernacular form of Henrik (see Henry 1).

variants.name: Harris
meanings: English (southern England and south Wales): from the personal name Harry + genitival -s. This surname is also established in Ireland, taken there principally during the Plantation of Ulster. However, in some cases, particularly in families coming from County Mayo, Harris can be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEarchadha. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

American shortened and altered form of Greek surnames begining with Cha(r)-, such as Chasandrinos (variant of Kassandrinos, a habitational name from the Kassandra peninsula of Chalkidiki), and various patronymics from the personal name Charalampos (see Charos ). In North America, the surname Harris may possibly also originate from a transferred use of the Greek personal (given) name Charis or Harris (shortened forms of Charalampos) as a surname (i.e. as a replacement of the original surname).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Enrique
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Santiago, Julio, Nelida, Raul, Alberto, Alejandro, Alejo, Alicia, Amalia, Andres.
Spanish: from the personal name Enrique, an equivalent of Henry .

variants.name: Endrizzi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Bruna, Ezio, Marino.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of a pet form of Endrigo, a variant of Enrico, Italian form of Henry .

variants.name: Enrico
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Domingo, Leoncio, Luis, Manuel, Melquiades, Nestor.  Italian Alberto, Aldo, Domenic, Gino, Livio, Pio.
Italian: from the personal name Enrico, Italian form of Henry 1.

variants.name: D'Errico
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Domenic, Salvatore, Angelo, Dante, Donato, Elio, Giampaolo, Giovanni, Marco, Matteo, Mauro.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name Errico, an equivalent of Henry . Compare Derico .

variants.name: Guzzo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Angelo, Salvatore, Agostino, Carmelina, Domenic, Gildo, Giorgio, Nunzio, Santo, Saverio.
Italian:
from a late medieval personal name Guzzo, a shortened pet form of various personal names, for example Arriguzzo, from Arrigo (see Henry ), and Uguzzo, from Ugo (see Hugh ).

variants.name: Harriott
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Donal, Donovan.
English (Sussex):
from the medieval personal names Henriot and Herriot, pet forms of Henry and Herry (see Harry ).

nameMeanings.name: Peter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a short form of any of the numerous names representing a transferred use of a surname formed with the Anglo-Norman French prefix fi(t)z ‘son of ’, as for example Fitzgerald , Fitzroy , or a transferred use of the surname Fitz, which may be of the same derivation, but applied originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same given name, or a local name from Fitz in Shropshire, named with the Old English personal name Fitt + hōh ‘hill spur’.

en.lifeSummary: Lord Peter Fitz Henry was born in 1128, in London, Middlesex, England as the son of Henry FitzAilwin Mayor of London and Margaret Fitz-Ailwin. He married Isabel De Chesney in 1178, in Addington, Surrey, England. They were the parents of at least 4 daughters. He died on 30 October 1207, in Addington, Surrey, England, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lord Peter Fitz Henry est né en 1128, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, fils de Henry FitzAilwin Mayor of London et Margaret Fitz-Ailwin. Il a épousé Isabel De Chesney en 1178, à Addington, Surrey, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 filles. Il est décédé le 30 octobre 1207, à Addington, Surrey, Angleterre, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lord Peter Fitz Henry wurde 1128 in London, Middlesex, England, als Sohn von Henry FitzAilwin Mayor of London und Margaret Fitz-Ailwin geboren. Er hat 1178 in Addington, Surrey, England Isabel De Chesney geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Töchter. Er starb am 30. Oktober 1207 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Addington, Surrey, England.

it.lifeSummary: Lord Peter Fitz Henry è nato il nel 1128 nel London, Middlesex, England come figlio di Henry FitzAilwin Mayor of London e Margaret Fitz-Ailwin. Lui sposato Isabel De Chesney nel 1178, nel Addington, Surrey, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figlie. Morì il 30 ottobre 1207, nel Addington, Surrey, England, all'età di 79 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord Peter Fitz Henryは1128年にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンでHenry FitzAilwin Mayor of LondonとMargaret Fitz-Ailwinの息子として生まれた。1178年にイギリススリーアッドイントンでIsabel De Chesneyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の娘の親であった。彼は1207年10月30日にイギリススリーアッドイントンで79歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord Peter Fitz Henry(은)는 1128년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 Henry FitzAilwin Mayor of London(와)과 Margaret Fitz-Ailwin의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1178년 영국 수리 애딩턴에서 Isabel De Chesney(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1207년 10월 30일 영국 수리 애딩턴에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lord Peter Fitz Henry nasceu em 1128, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra filho de Henry FitzAilwin Mayor of London e Margaret Fitz-Ailwin. Ele casou-se com Isabel De Chesney em 1178, em Addington, Surrey, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 30 de outubro de 1207, em Addington, Surrey, Inglaterra, com 79 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Lord Peter Fitz Henry родился в 1128 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия. Его родителей звали Henry FitzAilwin Mayor of London и Margaret Fitz-Ailwin. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel De Chesney в 1178 г. в местности аддингтон, сурри, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 дочери. Он умер 30 октября 1207 г. в местности аддингтон, сурри, Англия в возрасте 79 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Lord Peter Fitz Henry nació en 1128, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra como hijo de Henry FitzAilwin Mayor of London y Margaret Fitz-Ailwin. Se casó con Isabel De Chesney en 1178, en Addington, Surrey, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijas. Murió el 30 de octubre de 1207, en Addington, Surrey, Inglaterra, a la edad de 79 años.
